Title: The basal level of salicylic acid represses the PRT6 N-degron pathway to modulate root growth and stress response in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal root growth and abiotic stress response through repression of N-degron pathway
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased in Osaim1 roots and after AIP treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides N-terminal Met excision enabling OsPRT6 recognition of substrates such as OsWRKY62/76 and is upregulated when basal SA is reduced
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMetAP1A co-expression was required to detect OsPRT6 interaction with substrates in BiFC/co-IP assays. 2. Expression of OsMetAP genes was elevated in low-SA conditions. 3. Increased free N termini in Osaim1 support enhanced MetAP-associated pathway activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; BiFC; co-IP; free N-termini quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tuning point for N-degron activity, though likely pleiotropic because of broad N-terminal processing roles
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on proteome N-terminal processing may cause extensive pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper suggests OsMetAP1A can process Met-Asp N-termini, an unusual but functional feature in this system.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Peroxisomal AIM1-family enzyme involved in salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in AIM1-dependent salicylic acid biosynthesis required to maintain basal SA levels
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms or is required for biosynthesis of the target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains root meristem activity, root growth, ROS homeostasis, and abiotic stress tolerance in rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic mutant analysis of Osaim1; SA metabolite profiling; suppressor analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal growth; salt stress; AIP treatment as SA-biosynthesis inhibition context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of root tips, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1131484
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAIM1 is required to maintain basal salicylic acid levels in rice. 2. Loss of OsAIM1 causes reduced SA, short roots, altered ROS accumulation, and impaired abiotic stress responses. 3. Reduced SA in Osaim1 activates the OsPRT6 N-degron pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsAIM1 reduces basal SA accumulation and thereby impairs root growth and stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing AIM1-dependent basal SA biosynthesis could support root vigor and abiotic stress resilience in rice
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Osaim1 shows short-root defects and increased salt sensitivity when basal SA is reduced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsAIM1 is the causal upstream SA-biosynthetic gene defining the metabolic context, but the main experimental focus of this paper is on downstream OsPRT6 pathway regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: E3 ubiquitin ligase; N-degron pathway component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of OsWRKY62 and OsWRKY76 proteins, reducing their downstream effects on root growth and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salicylic-acid-linked signaling output affecting ROS homeostasis and stress response through protein turnover
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Basal salicylic acid represses OsPRT6 pathway activity; reduced SA releases this repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Basal salicylic acid level; SA depletion in Osaim1 or by AIP treatment activates pathway expression/activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports root growth, ROS balance, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsPRT6 partially rescues the short-root and salt-sensitive phenotypes of Osaim1 and enhances salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream executor repressed by basal SA; when activated under low SA, it destabilizes targets and contributes to defective root growth and stress response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous; expressed in roots, shoot bases, leaves, leaf sheaths, and panicles
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly in actively dividing cells such as root tips, young leaves, and lateral root primordia; expression increases in Osaim1 roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS suppressor mapping; MutMap; CRISPR knockout in Osaim1 background; co-segregation; qPCR; GUS expression; cell-free degradation assays; physiological salt-stress assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as N-recognin E3 ligase in the Arg/N-degron pathway rather than a stable named complex
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salt stress; low-SA condition in Osaim1; AIP treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProOsPRT6:GUS; native genomic locus; CRISPR-generated mutant alleles
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of root tips, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1131484
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPRT6 was identified as the causal suppressor locus Ossra1-1/OsSRA1-1. 2. Loss of OsPRT6 partially rescues Osaim1 root-growth defects without restoring SA levels. 3. Reduced basal SA elevates expression/activity of the OsPRT6 N-degron pathway. 4. OsPRT6 contributes to salt-stress sensitivity under low-SA conditions by destabilizing downstream substrates.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial suppression or tuning of OsPRT6 could be exploited to improve root growth and abiotic stress tolerance under low-SA or stress-prone conditions, but full developmental effects should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Osprt6 single mutants have shorter shoots; broad interference with proteolysis may cause pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PRT6 orthologs are conserved across green algae and land plants; this paper supports conservation of SA-mediated repression of the PRT6 pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein destabilization by OsPRT6 N-degron pathway; stabilization under reduced OsPRT6 function
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts downstream to regulate root growth and ROS-related responses, but direct target genes were not mapped in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salicylic-acid-linked signaling affecting ROS homeostasis and root growth
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Basal SA affects its stability indirectly by repressing OsPRT6 pathway activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports root growth and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a direct downstream target of OsPRT6; stabilization is inferred to contribute to root growth maintenance under adequate basal SA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supported downstream substrate connecting SA status to root-growth outputs via PRT6-dependent proteolysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC interaction assay; cell-free degradation assay with WT, Osprt6, Osaim1, and Osprt6 Osaim1 extracts; D2A stabilization mutant comparison
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY62 physically interacts with OsPRT6 in a MetAP1A-dependent manner. 2. The D2A substitution reduces OsPRT6 recognition and slows degradation. 3. OsWRKY62 behaves as a bona fide substrate of the OsPRT6 N-degron pathway linked to SA-regulated root growth.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering degradation-resistant alleles could help buffer root-growth defects under low-SA conditions, though broader defense/growth effects need evaluation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Protein was still partially degraded in Osprt6 extracts, implying parallel degradation systems and possible complexity in phenotype engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Second residue Asp is unusual for MetAP processing, suggesting low-efficiency but functional Met excision by OsMetAP1A in this context.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein destabilization by OsPRT6 N-degron pathway; D2A mutation prevents efficient recognition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts downstream to regulate root growth and ROS-related responses, but direct target genes were not identified in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salicylic-acid-linked signaling affecting ROS homeostasis and root growth
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Basal SA indirectly stabilizes OsWRKY76 by repressing OsPRT6 pathway activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports root growth and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Degradation-resistant OsWRKY76-D2A partially rescues the root growth defect of Osaim1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Validated direct downstream substrate that mediates part of the SA–PRT6 effect on root growth
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in root tips of OsWRKY76-D2A transgenic lines were similar to Osaim1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; co-IP; native-promoter transgenic expression of OsWRKY76-D2A in Osaim1; root phenotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OsWRKY76 promoter in pro:gn-OsWRKY76-D2A lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY76 physically interacts with OsPRT6 in a MetAP1A-dependent and residue-specific manner. 2. D2A substitution abolishes efficient OsPRT6 recognition. 3. Expression of degradation-resistant OsWRKY76-D2A partially rescues the Osaim1 short-root phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A degradation-resistant OsWRKY76 allele may be useful to improve root growth under reduced-SA conditions or stress, pending evaluation of pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue was observed, implying additional OsPRT6 substrates are involved
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsWRKY76 was previously implicated in root SA signaling and ROS accumulation; here it is positioned as a direct OsPRT6 substrate.
